info.name: Campaign Management System
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Campaign Management System.

**Base URL:** `http://localhost:5000/api`

**Setup:**
1. Make sure the redis based BullMQ is started first
2. Make sure the backend is running on port 5000
3. The `baseUrl` collection variable is pre-set to `http://localhost:5000/api`
4. After uploading contacts, copy a contact `_id` into the `contactId` variable
5. After creating a campaign, copy the campaign `_id` into the `campaignId` variable

**Response shape:**
```json
{ "success": true, "data": { ... }, "message": "...", "meta": null }
```

variable.description: Base URL of the backend API
Paste a contact _id here after fetching the contact list
Paste a campaign _id here after creating a campaign

item.name: Contacts
item.description: Endpoints for managing contacts - upload, list, search, and delete.

formdata.description: Select a .csv file from your computer. Required columns: name, email. Optional: phone, tags (semicolon-separated), plus any extra columns stored as metadata.

request.description:
Upload a CSV file to bulk-import contacts.

- Existing contacts matched by `email` are **updated** (upsert)
- New emails are **inserted**
- Rows missing `email` are skipped and reported in `errors`
- Extra CSV columns beyond `name, email, phone, tags` are stored in the contact's `metadata` field

**CSV column format:**
```
name,email,phone,tags,city,plan
Alice Johnson,alice@example.com,+1-555-0101,premium;active,New York,pro
```

`tags` must be semicolon-separated (`,` is the CSV delimiter).

query.description: Case-insensitive substring match on name or email
Comma-separated tags. Returns contacts matching ANY of these tags
ObjectId of the last item from the previous page. Omit for the first page
Sort direction: asc or desc (default: desc)

request.description:
Returns a cursor-paginated list of contacts.

**Pagination:** Use the `nextCursor` from the response as the `cursor` param in the next request. When `hasMore` is `false`, you've reached the last page.

**Tag filtering:** `tags=premium,active` returns contacts tagged with `premium` OR `active` (`$in` match).

**Search + tags together:** Both conditions are applied with `AND` logic - contacts must match both.

response.name: 200 OK - First page

request.description: Search contacts by name or email. The search is case-insensitive and matches substrings.

query.description: Returns contacts that have AT LEAST ONE of these tags

request.description: Filter contacts by tags. Comma-separated list. Uses MongoDB `$in` - contacts having any of the listed tags are returned.

Returns the total number of contacts in the database.

Uses `estimatedDocumentCount()` - reads from MongoDB collection metadata. O(1) operation, no collection scan regardless of dataset size.

Fetch a single contact by its MongoDB ObjectId.

**Setup:** Set the `contactId` collection variable to a valid contact `_id` before sending.

Permanently delete a contact by ID.

⚠️ Does **not** cascade-delete associated DeliveryLogs - existing campaign logs for this contact remain in the database.

**Setup:** Set the `contactId` collection variable before sending.

item.name: Campaigns
item.description: Endpoints for creating and managing messaging campaigns.

request.description:
Create a new campaign.

**What happens on creation:**
1. Audience is resolved immediately - contacts matching `audienceFilter` are found
2. One `DeliveryLog` with `status: queued` is created per matching contact
3. Campaign is saved with `status: draft` and `totalContacts` set
4. Campaign must be explicitly started via `POST /campaigns/:id/start`

**`audienceFilter` rules:**
- Omit entirely → targets **all** contacts
- `tags` only → contacts having ANY of the listed tags
- `search` only → contacts whose name or email contains the search string
- Both `tags` + `search` → contacts must satisfy **both** conditions (AND logic)

**`messageTemplate`:** Use `user` as a personalization placeholder for the contact's name.

response.name: 201 Created
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Campaign created",
  "data": {
    "_id": "64f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b9c0d2",
    "name": "Summer Sale Announcement",
    "messageTemplate": "Hi user, check out our summer deals!",
    "status": "draft",
    "audienceFilter": {
      "tags": ["premium"],
      "search": ""
    },
    "totalContacts": 5000,
    "sentCount": 0,
    "failedCount": 0,
    "pendingCount": 5000,
    "scheduledAt": null,
    "completedAt": null,
    "createdAt": "2024-01-15T11:00:00.000Z",
    "updatedAt": "2024-01-15T11:00:00.000Z"
  },
  "meta": null
}

response.name: 400 Bad Request - Missing fields

request.description: Create a campaign with no `audienceFilter` - targets every contact in the database.

query.description: Filter by status: draft | running | completed | paused | failed
ObjectId of the last campaign from the previous page

request.description:
Returns a cursor-paginated list of campaigns sorted by `createdAt` descending (newest first).

The frontend polls this endpoint every 5 seconds to show live status updates.

response.name: 200 OK

query.description: Allowed values: draft | running | completed | paused | failed

request.description: Filter campaigns by their current status. Useful for showing only active campaigns on the dashboard.

Returns aggregated statistics across all campaigns plus the total contact count.

**Performance note:** Powered by a single MongoDB `$group` aggregation on the Campaign collection - reads pre-computed `sentCount`, `failedCount`, `pendingCount` counters. Does **not** scan the DeliveryLog collection. O(campaigns) not O(messages).

The frontend polls this endpoint every 5 seconds.

Fetch a single campaign document by its MongoDB ObjectId.

**Setup:** Set the `campaignId` collection variable to a valid campaign `_id` before sending.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Campaign fetched",
  "data": {
    "_id": "64f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b9c0d2",
    "name": "Summer Sale Announcement",
    "messageTemplate": "Hi user, check out our summer deals!",
    "status": "completed",
    "audienceFilter": { "tags": ["premium"], "search": "" },
    "totalContacts": 5000,
    "sentCount": 4510,
    "failedCount": 490,
    "pendingCount": 0,
    "scheduledAt": null,
    "completedAt": "2024-01-15T11:12:00.000Z",
    "createdAt": "2024-01-15T11:00:00.000Z",
    "updatedAt": "2024-01-15T11:12:00.000Z"
  },
  "meta": null
}

request.description:
Start a campaign that is currently in `draft` status.

**What happens:**
1. Campaign `status` is set to `running`
2. A BullMQ job `{ campaignId }` is enqueued in Redis with `jobId: campaign-{id}` (prevents duplicate jobs)
3. API returns **immediately** - processing is fully asynchronous
4. The worker picks up the job and processes contacts in batches of 500

**Constraints:**
- Only campaigns in `draft` status can be started
- Calling this on a `running` or `completed` campaign returns `400`

**Setup:** Set the `campaignId` collection variable to a `draft` campaign `_id`.

response.name: 400 Bad Request - Already running

request.description:
Returns the full campaign document, a live status breakdown from DeliveryLogs, and a timeline of message activity grouped by minute.

**Three queries run in parallel (`Promise.all`):**
1. `Campaign.findById` - the campaign document
2. `DeliveryLog.aggregate $group by status` - live counts per status
3. `DeliveryLog.aggregate $group by status + minute` - timeline for charting

The frontend polls this endpoint every 5 seconds to update the progress bar and stat cards on the campaign detail page.

**Setup:** Set the `campaignId` collection variable.

response.name: 200 OK

query.description: Filter by delivery status: queued | processing | sent | failed
ObjectId of the last log from the previous page

request.description:
Returns a cursor-paginated list of DeliveryLogs for a campaign.

Each log has `contactId` **populated** with `name`, `email`, and `phone` - no separate contact lookup needed.

**Common use cases:**
- `?status=failed` - show only failed deliveries for retry analysis
- `?status=sent` - verify sent messages
- No `status` param → all logs

**Setup:** Set the `campaignId` collection variable.

response.name: 200 OK - All logs
200 OK - Failed logs only

request.description:
Permanently delete a campaign and **all its associated DeliveryLogs**.

⚠️ This is a cascading delete - all DeliveryLogs for this campaign are removed first, then the campaign document is deleted.

⚠️ Can be called on any campaign regardless of status. Deleting a `running` campaign will leave a job in the BullMQ queue - the worker will fail gracefully when it can't find the campaign.

**Setup:** Set the `campaignId` collection variable.
